LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS DULER & COMPANY AND RISK MANAGEMENT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE REMAINS A FACTUAL ISSUE WHETHER PLAINTIFF'S WORK OUTSIDE THE FEDERAL ENCLAVE, WHICH FORMED PART OF THE BASIS FOR DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT, WAS SUFFICIENT TO PERMIT APPLICATION OF STATE LAW AS TO AT LEAST SOME PORTION OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: JACQUELINE CHAN CSR # 10276

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Anne Soter, Esq. of Curiale, Hirschfeld & Kraemer, 415-835-9000 for defendants and moving parties Duler & Co and Risk Management
Stephen Gorski, Esq. and Sara Taylor, Esq., 415-248-1010 for plaintiff and opposing party Paul Louis Walker

RULING:
ARGUED and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows --
DEFENDANTS DULER & COMPANY AND RISK MANAGEMENT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE REMAINS A FACTUAL ISSUE WHETHER PLAINTIFF'S WORK OUTSIDE THE FEDERAL ENCLAVE, WHICH FORMED PART OF THE BASIS FOR DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT, WAS SUFFICIENT TO PERMIT APPLICATION OF STATE LAW AS TO AT LEAST SOME PORTION OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Jacqueline Chan CSR # 10276

MISCELLANEOUS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-05-2009 Text Ruling
